prompt: I'm writing C bindings to BlockSci (the deployment indexes Bitcoin), a high-performance blockchain analytics engine that exposes a C++ iterator over blocks. I want C users to be able to run a piece of their own code for each block as the iterator walks the collection. The collection has on the order of 800 million blocks, and this path is performance-critical, so the per-block step is hot. For inspiration, think about how libraries like Polars or pandas let users express per-row work. Please design the binding and give me the public C header and the wrapper as a single self-contained unit.
